title: Alaska Psychiatric Association Annual CME Conference 2026
description: When: April 10-11, 2026 Where: Aloft Hotel, 310 W 36th Avenue, Anchorage, AK 99503 Continuing Education: Available, see event website for more details. For more information, including registration, visit the event website here . Join us for the Alaska Psychiatric Association's 27th Annual CME Conference, taking place April 10–11, 2026 in Anchorage, featuring expert speakers from leading institutions across the country. This year's program covers timely and essential topics including disaster mental health, eating disorders, climate change and psychiatry, sleep medicine, generational differences in healthcare, and the latest research in depression treatment and psychopharmacology.
host_organization: University of Alaska Anchorage Continuing Studies

title: ALPHA Webinar - Understanding Dementia: Public Health Interventions for Brain Health
description: When: April 8, 2026 | 12:00pm Where: Online via Zoom For more information, including registration, visit the event website here. Join us for an overview of dementia and discover evidence-based strategies to lower risk and protect brain health in your practice. Speakers include: Mara McKown, MPH, MSW, CPH Mara McKown is currently an evaluator and epidemiologist in the State of Alaska’s Section of Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion. Mara focuses on dementia, cardiovascular health, cancer, and diabetes. Mara also is an adjunct faculty member at Alaska Pacific University teaching a class on epidemiology and research methods for environmental public health and health scientist majors. Before moving to Alaska, Mara received a Master’s of Public Health and a Master’s of Social Work from Washington University in St. Louis. Stephanie Smith, MFA, NCG Stephanie Smith, MFA, NCG is a Public Health Specialist 2 and the Program Manager for the Dementia Education and Prevention Program with the State of Alaska’s Division of Public Health. As the state subject matter expert on Alzheimer’s Disease and Related Dementias, she brings nearly 10 years of experience working directly with individuals experiencing dementia and intellectual and developmental disabilities. Her background includes roles as a public guardian and a program manager at The Arc of Anchorage. She has a Master of Fine Arts Degree from the University of Alaska and is a National Certified Guardian.
host_organization: University of Alaska Anchorage Continuing Studies

title: Coronal Polishing Course - March 2026
description: Welcome Dental Professionals! We now have the March 2026 Coronal Polishing course officially built in the UAOnline system. Below are the course details: Coronal Polishing Course: - Eligibility: Must have at least six months of chairside experience. - Course Structure: Four-day course, including two online lecture sessions and two in-person lab sessions. - Lecture (Virtual via Zoom): March 24 & 26, from 5:30 PM – 8:30 PM (Dates/times could be subject to change) - Lab (In-Person): March 28 - 29, from 8:30 AM – 2:30 PM (Dates/times could be subject to change) - Location: UAA Dental Clinic, 3500 Seawolf Dr, Allied Health Bldg, Ste 131, Anchorage, AK 99508. - Cost: $375 (includes registration). Does not cover food, lodging, parking, or transportation. Please adhere to the following steps to ensure proper registration: - Apply to UAA and become a non-degree-seeking student. If you've been a UAA student before but haven't taken a UAA course for five or more semesters, please apply as a Returning Student . If you have any difficulty applying, please contact the Admissions Office . - Obtain your UA email, UAOnline login, and Student ID #. - Email uaa_dentalprograms@alaska.edu your name and Student ID # so that our Program Support Specialist knows to grant you the required departmental approval to take the course. - Monitor your UA email closely for a notification email indicating that you have been granted departmental approval and are then able to proceed to register for the class. - Register via UAOnline to DH A590-201 titled " Coronal Polishing for Dental Pros ". The easiest way to search is using the CRN which is 36918. We require a minimum of 10 students registered in order to move forward with the course.
host_organization: University of Alaska Anchorage Continuing Studies

title: Ethics in DSM Practice - April 6 2026 - Online
description: Continuing Education: 3 Credit Hours Available. See training for more details. Vanessa Meade, PsyD, LCSW (she/her), is an assistant professor at the University of Alaska Anchorage's School of Social Work. She is also a U.S. Army/Gulf War Veteran and a former Alaska State Trooper. Dr. Meade is a licensed clinical social worker and psychologist in Alaska. She has also worked as a clinician/therapist in rural community mental health agencies and juvenile justice. She is a certified mindfulness teacher and facilitates mindfulness-based programs at UAA with students, faculty, and staff, as well as with military veterans and wildland firefighters through online and multi-day residential retreat programs throughout the United States. She serves on several state and national advisory committees and boards. Dr. Meade also focuses on equity and social justice in her work, including within the LGBTQ+ community. View the CACHE Refund Policy here.

title: Mental Health First Aid for Older Adults - March 23 2026 - Fairbanks
description: When: Monday, March 23, 2026 - 8am-5pm Where: Multimedia Room at Tanana Valley Clinic, 1001 Noble St, Fairbanks AK 99701 Join the UAA College of Health to attend Older Adult Mental Health First Aid training. This class will teach you how to identify, understand, and respond to signs of mental illness and substance use disorders with a focus on older adults. This 8 hour training gives you the skills you need to reach out and provide initial support to someone who may be developing a mental health or substance use problem and help connect them to appropriate care. Join us Monday, March 23rd 2026 from 8am-5pm in Fairbanks! By registering here you will be receiving access to the MHFA Connect portal and additional details regarding the course.

title: Transforming Touch - Part 1 - April 8-13, 2026
description: Where: For more information, including registration, visit the website here. Deepen your trauma-informed healing practice. Transforming Touch® is a gentle, body-based approach (therapy) that uses safe, attuned touch to help heal early developmental trauma and strengthen secure attachment. Participants will learn Dr. Terrell's 7-Point Protocol, a powerful method for supporting regulation, connection, and healing in clients with trauma-related issues. This is a one-time, two-part training opportunity for professionals and practitioners interested in deepening their trauma-informed healing practice. If you know someone who feels called to help others heal, this training is for them. *For questions or registration assistance, contact Kathie at kkalanickse@gmail.com .
host_organization: University of Alaska Anchorage Continuing Studies
